hero.headline: Elmar Haneveld
hero.subtitle: I work at the intersection of business, design, systems and people. For 20+ years I've been turning strategy into design systems and experiences that scale — connecting what organizations need with what people actually value.

about.headline: Business, design,
paragraphs: Great design doesn't happen in isolation. For over 20 years, I've worked across four dimensions: understanding the **business**, refining the **design**, scaling through **systems**, and always centering the **people** who use what we make.

As an art director and design system architect from the Netherlands, I bridge the gap between strategy and execution. I translate business goals into design direction, build token-driven systems that scale across teams, and keep the design sharp — from concept to pixel.

What sets my work apart is the ability to connect disciplines. I speak the language of developers, understand the priorities of stakeholders, and advocate for the end user. The best outcomes happen when business, design, systems, and people move together.
about.personal: When I'm not designing, you'll find me doing CrossFit, exploring existential philosophy, playing guitar, or hiking with my family. I intentionally keep my phone on mute.

clientProjects.title: Polestar
clientProjects.description: Collaborated with the design system team to develop reusable components for digital applications. Aligning design feedback, implementing UX best practices, and delivering quality designs based on strict brand guidelines.
clientProjects.challenge: Polestar's digital ecosystem was expanding rapidly across web, app, and internal tools — but the design language was fragmenting. Teams worked in silos, components were duplicated with inconsistencies, and there was no shared source of truth connecting design and development.
clientProjects.solution: I joined the design system team to create a unified, token-driven component library. Each component was built to strict brand standards while remaining flexible enough for diverse use cases. I established a feedback loop between designers and developers, ensuring every component was both pixel-perfect and technically sound.

clientProjects.title: TomTom
clientProjects.description: Designed the user interface for a fitness watch with a 3-bit screen — making something meaningful for athletes on a tiny surface with a palette of just 8 colors.
clientProjects.challenge: How do you design a compelling fitness experience on a screen that can only display 8 colors? The TomTom fitness watch had extreme technical limitations — a 3-bit display, minimal processing power, and interaction limited to a single button. Every pixel and every state had to be deliberate.
clientProjects.solution: I approached the constraints as a creative opportunity. By stripping the interface down to its essence, I created a visual language that was instantly readable at a glance — critical for athletes mid-workout. Bold, high-contrast layouts with purposeful use of the 8-color palette made the watch feel premium despite its technical limitations.

clientProjects.title: Air France KLM
clientProjects.description: Design system work and digital experience design for one of Europe's leading airline groups.
clientProjects.challenge: Air France-KLM operates multiple brands across dozens of digital touchpoints — web, app, kiosks, and internal tools. The challenge was creating a design system flexible enough to serve both Air France and KLM's distinct brand identities while maintaining consistency and efficiency at scale.
clientProjects.solution: I worked on a multi-brand design system architecture using design tokens as the foundation. Brand-specific tokens allowed the same components to express different visual identities, while shared structural tokens ensured consistency in spacing, typography, and interaction patterns. The system dramatically reduced design debt and sped up delivery.

clientProjects.title: E.ON Drive
clientProjects.description: Digital product design for E.ON's electric vehicle charging platform, creating intuitive experiences for the future of mobility.
clientProjects.challenge: Electric vehicle charging is still unfamiliar territory for most drivers. E.ON needed a digital experience that made finding, using, and paying for charging stations as simple as refueling — while handling complex scenarios like different connector types, pricing models, and charging speeds.
clientProjects.solution: I designed the end-to-end charging experience with a focus on reducing cognitive load. The interface surfaces only what's relevant at each step: nearby stations when searching, real-time availability when arriving, and clear progress when charging. A clean visual hierarchy guides users through what could be an overwhelming process.

clientProjects.title: Centraal Beheer
clientProjects.description: Creative concept and design work for one of the Netherlands' most recognizable insurance brands.
clientProjects.challenge: Centraal Beheer is famous for its "Even Apeldoorn bellen" campaigns — but translating that playful, human brand voice into digital experiences was the real challenge. Insurance is inherently complex and often perceived as boring. The digital presence needed to feel as warm and approachable as the brand's iconic advertising.
clientProjects.solution: I developed creative concepts that brought Centraal Beheer's human touch into every digital interaction. By focusing on storytelling, clear language, and moments of delight, we transformed insurance interfaces from dry form-filling into experiences that felt genuinely helpful. The design language balanced professionalism with the brand's signature warmth.

personalVentures.title: Vrank
personalVentures.description: A mobile app designed to spark engaging, meaningful conversations. From concept to launch — bringing people closer through the art of asking better questions.
personalVentures.challenge: In an age of surface-level digital interactions, real conversations are becoming rare. People want deeper connections but don't always know how to start. The challenge was creating something that felt natural — not forced or gamified — that genuinely brings people closer through conversation.
personalVentures.solution: Vrank is a card-based conversation app with carefully curated questions designed to go beyond small talk. I designed the entire experience from brand identity to interaction design, focusing on tactile, satisfying gestures and a warm visual language. The app creates a ritual around conversation — making it feel like opening a beautiful deck of cards, not using another app.

personalVentures.title: Makuuchi
personalVentures.description: A beautifully designed app for sumo wrestling fans, bringing the ancient sport to a modern digital audience.
personalVentures.challenge: Sumo wrestling has a rich 2000-year history, but its digital presence is fragmented and often inaccessible to international fans. Tournament data, wrestler profiles, and historical records exist across scattered Japanese websites with no unified, modern experience.
personalVentures.solution: I designed and built Makuuchi as a love letter to sumo — combining deep respect for the sport's tradition with modern interface design. Rich data visualizations bring tournament brackets and wrestler statistics to life, while the visual design references traditional Japanese aesthetics without becoming kitsch. Every detail, from typography to data presentation, serves both hardcore fans and newcomers.

personalVentures.title: CreateNew
personalVentures.description: A creative venture exploring new ways to bring ideas to life through design and technology.
personalVentures.challenge: The gap between having a creative idea and actually bringing it to life is where most projects die. CreateNew was born from the belief that the tools, processes, and mindset for turning ideas into reality shouldn't be gatekept — they should be accessible and enjoyable.
personalVentures.solution: I developed the brand identity and creative direction for CreateNew — a venture focused on making the creative process more accessible. The brand language is deliberately open-ended and inviting, using generative design elements that change and evolve, reflecting the nature of creativity itself. From logo system to digital presence, every touchpoint embodies the idea that everyone can create something new.

roles.description: Leading design system work for one of Europe's largest airline groups.
Part of the web design system team — aligning design feedback, implementing UX best practices, and developing reusable components based on strict brand guidelines.
Designed the user interface for TomTom's fitness watch. Created meaningful experiences for athletes within extreme technical constraints.
Digital product design for E.ON's electric vehicle charging platform.
Creative concept and digital design for one of the Netherlands' most well-known insurance brands.

contact.headline: Let's create
contact.description: Available for design system consulting, art direction, and creative collaboration. Based in the Netherlands, working globally.
